Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021 
Error processing SSI file 
Gonococcal Infections Among Neonates 
Print 
Minus 
Related Pages 
Prenatal screening and treatment of pregnant women for gonorrhea is the best method for preventing N. gonorrhoeae infection among neonates. Gonococcal infection among neonates results from perinatal exposure to the mother’s infected cervix. It is usually an acute illness that manifests 2–5 days after birth. Prevalence of infection among neonates depends on the prevalence of infection among pregnant women and whether pregnant women are screened and treated for gonorrhea during pregnancy.
